task_name: Add Custom Theme System to T3 Code Editor

plan_metrics.plan_content:
Here's the complete plan I've designed after a thorough exploration of the codebase:

---

## What I found

- **`hooks/useTheme.ts`** already handles dark/light/system mode via `useSyncExternalStore` — this is the exact pattern to replicate for the new hook (and it stays **unchanged**)
- **`index.css`** uses Tailwind v4 with CSS custom properties (`--background`, `--foreground`, `--accent`, etc.) — inline `style.setProperty()` overrides take precedence over stylesheet vars, which is how the color themes will apply
- **`_chat.settings.tsx`** has the existing radio-button UI pattern (bordered rows with "Selected" badge) to reuse for the ThemeSelector
- **`packages/shared`** uses plain TypeScript subpath exports — no Effect schema needed, just add a `./themes` entry

---

## Plan: 7 files touched

| # | File | Change |
|---|------|--------|
| 1 | `packages/shared/src/themes.ts` | **Create** — `ThemeColors`, `Theme` interfaces, 3 built-ins, `getTheme()`, `listThemes()` |
| 2 | `packages/shared/package.json` | **Add** `"./themes"` subpath export |
| 3 | `apps/web/src/hooks/useColorTheme.ts` | **Create** — `useSyncExternalStore` hook, applies CSS vars + toggles `.dark`, storage key `t3code:color-theme:v1`, module-load init to prevent flash |
| 4 | `apps/web/src/index.css` | **Add** `--sidebar-bg`, `--editor-bg`, `--terminal-bg`, `--status-bar-bg` vars with light/dark defaults |
| 5 | `apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx` | **Create** — radio rows with 3 color swatches per theme |
| 6 | `apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` | **Add** `ThemeSelector` in Appearance section above the existing dark/light/system group |
| 7 | `apps/web/src/main.tsx` | **Add** side-effect `import "~/hooks/useColorTheme"` so the theme applies before React's first paint |

Key design decision: `useColorTheme` applies the CSS custom property overrides **and** toggles `.dark` (each theme has an `isDark: boolean`). The inline `style.setProperty` calls take precedence over the CSS file's `:root` declarations, so `t3-midnight` naturally overrides the default palette. No new dependencies, no build config changes.
